G Generation Series

A half-century has passed since humanity began emigrating into outer space due to overpopulation.
Hundreds of gigantic space colonies now orbit Earth, humans living along the inner walls of the colonies' main cylinders.
With almost half of humankind residing in space, these colonies have become humanity's second home where people are born, raised...and die.
It is January 1, U.C. 0079.
The furthest cluster of colonies from Earth, Side 3, has proclaimed itself the Principality of Zeon and has launched a war of independence against the Earth Federation.
In just one month of fighting, war casualties have halved their respective populations. Humanity is disgusted at the atrocities they themselves have wrought.
Eight months have passed since the rebellion began. They were at a stalemate...

U.C. 0068, the Autonomous Republic of Munzo, Side 3.
Zeon Zum Deikun, who preached of a human reformation for those who had ventured into space, attempted to declare complete independence from the Earth Federation. While giving a speech at a congressional podium, he suddenly collapsed and departed this world.
After Deikun's death, his close associate Jimba Ral believed—and insisted, publicly—that his death was a Zabi family plot.
However, doing so only hastened the Degwin Sodo Zabi-led Zabi family's machinations to take control of Side 3 and Munzo.
As the previously unspoken history of this uprising is revealed, Casval and Artesia, the orphaned children of Deikun, are powerless in the face of the fate that awaits them, one forged in the fires of turbulent times...

MSD is an abbreviation of Mobile Suit Discovery. 
This refers to mobile suit variations that appear in MOBILE SUIT GUNDAM THE ORIGIN.
An abbreviation of "Mobile Suit Variation," MSV is a series of variant designs for the mobile suits that appeared in the animated TV show, "Mobile Suit Gundam."

This sequel to MSV is an additional series of alternate mobile suits to those that appeared in the animated TV show "Mobile Suit Gundam."
MS-X is an abbreviation of "Mobile Suit Pezun."

A Sunrise planned and produced movie that premiered at the Bandai Museum on July 19th, 2004. 
It was a 3D CG movie set during the same One Year War period shown in ""Mobile Suit Gundam,"" but told from the perspective of the Principality of Zeon.
In 2005, Kadokawa Shoten published the novel and manga ""Mobile Suit Gundam MS IGLOO 603"" based on this story.

A manga adaptation of The Hidden One Year War.
January, U.C. 0079. Engineering Lieutenant Oliver May of the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit witnesses the now historic and pivotal Battle of Loum...

An OVA planned and produced by Sunrise, released on October 24, 2008. 
This 3D CG piece is set during the same One Year War period as the TV show ""Mobile Suit Gundam,"" and is told from the perspective of the Earth Federation.
In 2009, Kadokawa Shoten released a manga based on this work.

An OVA planned and produced by Sunrise, released on January 25th, 1996. 
This animated series takes place during the One Year War period depicted in the TV anime ""Mobile Suit Gundam,"" and tells the story of young officer Shiro Amada.
On August 1st, 1998, the animated movie ""Mobile Suit Gundam: The 08th MS Team Miller's Report"" was given a theatrical release.
In 1999, Kadokawa Shoten released a novel and manga based on this work.

An OVA planned and produced by Sunrise, released on March 23rd, 1989. 
A small- scale war story taking place during the One Year War featured in the TV show ""Mobile Suit Gundam."" 
It centers on an encounter between Bernard Wiseman, a rookie Zeon soldier, and Alfred Izuruha, a boy living at Side 6's Libot Colony.
In October 1989, Kadokawa Shoten released a novel based on this work.

A war simulation game released by Bandai on February 10th, 1995. 
It has two main gameplay modes. 
One mode gives players the chance to replay the story of "Mobile Suit Gundam;" the other presents an original story called "A Prayer for the Dying."

A trilogy of 3D shooting games released by Bandai. 
"Mobile Suit Gundam Side Story I: Blue Terror" was released on September 20th, 1996.
"Mobile Suit Gundam Side Story II: Heir to the Blue" followed on December 6th, 1996 and "Mobile Suit Gundam Side Story III: The Condemned" completed the set on March 7th, 1997.
In 1997, Kodansha released a novel and manga based on this work.

The Blue Reaper returns once again! A new "blue" installment told by an all-star team! In the final days of the One Year War of U.C. 0079, the 11th Independent Mechanized Composite Corps, otherwise known as the Guinea Pig Team, is formed with a view to
collecting real mobile suit combat data. 
An encounter with a blue mobile suit sets the team's destiny in motion in this manga adaptation of the masterful video game.

Released by Bandai on August 1st, 2002, this 3D action game allows players to fight for either the Federation or Principality of Zeon.
This choice affects the game's available stages.
In 2002, Kadokawa Shoten released a novel and manga based on this work.

An original story first appearing in a dramatic and high-speed action game released by Bandai Namco on May 29th, 2014.
 The game also contained six previously released Gundam side stories, in addition to "Mobile Suit Gundam Side Story: Missing Link."
On February 26th, 2014, Kadokawa Shoten released a manga based on the work in its magazine "Gundam Ace."

U.C. 0079. Nearly one year has passed since the war between the Earth Federation Forces and the Principality of Zeon began.
The Side 4 space colony cluster, Moore, has been destroyed in a Zeon attack. The loss of life was enormous. The clouds of drifting debris from the colony and wrecked ships resemble forlorn spirits of the dead.
Moore has been reduced to nothing more than a perilous shoal zone. Collisions among the debris generates electricity, emitting constant arcs of lightning.
No one knows who came up with the new name for the region, but it eventually sticks. It becomes known by all as Thunderbolt Sector.

A squad-based action game released by Bandai Namco on September 3rd, 2009. 
A Sunrise-produced OVA prologue was included as a special feature.
Between 2009 and 2011, Kadokawa Shoten released a manga based on this work.

Amate Yuzuriha is a high-school student living peacefully in a space colony floating in outer space.
When she meets a war refugee named Nyaan, Amate is drawn into the illegal mobile suit dueling sport known as Clan Battle.
Under the entry name ""Machu,"" she throws herself into fierce battle day after day, piloting the GQuuuuuuX. Then an unidentified Gundam mobile suit pursued by both the space force and the police appears before her, along with its pilot, a boy named Shuji.
Now their world is about to enter a new era.

An OVA planned and produced by Sunrise, released on May 22nd, 1991.
The story follows young officer Kou Uraki three years after the One Year War of ""Mobile Suit Gundam.""
The OVA was followed on August 29th, 1992 with the theatrical release of ""Mobile Suit Gundam 0083: The Afterglow Of Zeon.""
In 1992, Kadokawa Shoten released a novel based on this work.

Gundam 0083 as told from a new perspective! Following the end of the One Year War, the Zeon remnants lie dormant within the Earth Sphere. 
The ultimate motives of the so-called Delaz Fleet remain unclear as it continues conducting clandestine maneuvers in an attempt to revive Zeon! 
A manga adaptation by Masato Natsumoto.

This official Sunrise mobile suit setting collection, released by Ascii Media Works in 2002, is set during the time period of "Mobile Suit Gundam 0083: Stardust Memory." In 2011, Ascii Media Works released a number of new setting materials in its "Mobile Suit Gundam Secret Weapons: Phantom Bullets - Planning & Design Materials."

From Hajime Katoki comes a new mobile suit, deployed in battle during U.C. 0084. The Striker Custom, newly-designed by Hajime Katoki, steps onto the battlefield in U.C. 0084. A manga launched in collaboration with Bonds of the Battlefield and Gundam Ace, and written by Sono Yoshihiro of ""We're Federation Hooligans!!"" fame.

A Gundam manga drawn by Haruhiko Mikimoto, character designer of War in the Pocket! The story of "ugly duckling," Asuna Elmarit, and model student, Erisia Nocton. A tale of dreams and setbacks, talent and romance, and of hopes unfulfilled... Two girls at the whim of their fates as Newtypes!

First appearing in Ascii Media Works' "Dengeki Hobby Magazine" in September 2002, this novel tells stories from the Titans' perspective during the period  depicted in the TV anime "Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam."
A manga based on this work was released by Ascii Media Works in 2003.

A TV show planned and produced by Sunrise and directed by original "Mobile Suit Gundam" director Yoshiyuki Tomino.
First broadcast on March 2nd, 1985, the story takes place seven years after the One Year War and follows the story of Kamille Bidan, a youth living in Side 7's Green Noa colony.
In 1985, Kodansha released a novel based on this work.

An animated trilogy planned and produced by Sunrise and directed by Yoshiyuki Tomino. 
It premiered on May 28th, 2005. 
Celebrating the 20th anniversary of "Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam," it was a reboot created by Yoshiyuki Tomino himself.

From Hiroyuki Kitazume, animation director of the TV anime Mobile Suit Z Gundam, which was announced in the Kadokawa Shoten-published magazine Gundam Ace, comes a serialized retelling of Mobile Suit Z Gundam from the perspective of Quattro Bajeena, aka Char Aznable.
A variation series for the mobile suits appearing in the TV anime  "Mobile Suit Z Gundam."

Planned and produced by Sunrise, this digital theatrical release based on the world of ""Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam"" was created to be viewed in planetarium facilities.
It has screened several times after first airing on August 10th, 2001.

Appearing in Kaiga's Model Graphics magazine starting in September 1987, this serial and its later novel compilation are set in the time between the animated TV shows ""Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam"" and ""Mobile Suit Gundam ZZ.""
In 1990, a complete novel featuring additional material was released as Gundam ""Sentinel: Alice's Confession.""

A TV animated show planned and produced by Sunrise. It premiered on March 1st, 1986, and was directed by Yoshiyuki Tomino, of the original "Mobile Suit Gundam" series.
It tells the story of Judau Ashta, a boy living in the Side 1 colony Shangri-La.
A sequel to the TV show "Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam," it takes place during the period after the Gryps Conflict.
In 1986, Kodansha released a novel based on this work.

Published in the Kadokawa Shoten-published magazine "Gundam Ace" and presented by mecha designer Kunio Okawara, this is a variation series for the mobile suits that appeared in the TV anime "Mobile Suit Gundam."

Published in the Kadokawa Shoten-published magazine Gundam Ace, this manga is based on the mobile suit variation series MSV-R.
It first saw print on April 26th, 2010.

A story set in U.C. 0089 that follows protagonist Mashiro Okusu,thought to bear some connection to the Newtype once known as "The Man From Jupiter," Paptimus Scirocco, as he is embroiled in a new conflict after encountering the mysterious Gundam Over.on.

Set in U.C. 0090, a year after the First Neo Zeon War came to an end, Darry Neil Guns uses scrap parts to build the work-ready D Gundam while working at the Colony Public Corporation's subcontractor, Monotone Mouse (MM). 
After using his Gundam to intervene during a battle between the Federation and the terrorist group Colored, Darry finds himself dragged into their conflict.

A theatrical release planned and produced by Sunrise, directed by Yoshiyuki Tomino, and premiered on March 12th, 1988. 
This work depicts the final battle between fated rivals Amuro Ray and Char Aznable.
In December 1987, Tokuma Shoten released the novel "Mobile Suit Gundam: Char's Counterattack." On February 20th, 1988, Kadokawa Shoten released "Mobile Suit Gundam: Char's Counterattack - Beltorchika's Children."
A variation series for the mobile suits appearing in the theatrical release, "Mobile Suit Gundam: Char's Counterattack."

Published in the Bandai magazine SD Club and presented by mecha designer Kunio Okawara, this is a variation series for the mobile suits appearing in the TV show "Mobile Suit Gundam."
M-MSV is short for "Missing Mobile Suit Variation."

A novel by Harutoshi Fukui published in the Kadokawa Shoten magazine "Gundam Ace."
The story focuses on Banagher Links, a student at the Anaheim Electronics Industrial College in the space colony Industrial 7. 
It is set three years after the events of "Mobile Suit Gundam: Char's Counterattack."
The story first saw print in "Gundam Ace" in February 2007.  
Later, the Sunrise-planned and produced OVA, "Mobile Suit Gundam UC," was announced, and on February 20th, 2010, the first episode "Day of the Unicorn," premiered at a special screening. 
The seventh and final episode, "Over the Rainbow" was released on May 17th, 2014.
A variation series for the mobile suits appearing in the OVA "Mobile Suit Gundam UC."

The ultimate destination of the Newtype myth... Set during the Universal Century, a new saga depicting the events that follow the smash hit series Mobile Suit Gundam Unicorn was unveiled in cinemas on November 30th, 2018. Is this a tale of destruction, or of eternal life?

Twelve years have passed since the Second Neo Zeon War, also known as Char's Rebellion.
U.C. 0105... The corruption within the Earth Federation has accelerated the contamination of the planet, leading to the inhumane "Manhunt" policy forcibly relocating civilians to space.
The anti-Earth Federation movement Mafty begins fighting back by brutally assassinating high-ranking government officials.
Its leader goes by the name of Mafty Navue Erin, but his true identity is that of Hathaway Noa, son of Bright Noa—the Federation Forces captain who fought in the One Year War.
Hathaway strives to carve his own path as a warrior, guided by the philosophies, ideals, and wills of Amuro Ray and Char Aznable. His destiny takes a sharp turn, however, when he crosses paths with Captain Kenneth Sleg of the Federation Forces and the mysterious girl Gigi Andalucia.

The year is U.C. 0120. The Federation has begun development of smaller mobile suits, with the new S.N.R.I.-developed F90 models undergoing extensive test operations. During one such test, however, the Zeon Remnants' Oldsmobile forces launch an assault on an experimental unit and steal the Unit 2 prototype.
Attempting to suppress Oldsmobile, the Federation dispatches a fleet to Mars, the latter's base of operations. Accompanying the fleet is Def, an F90 test pilot. However, faced with both the appearance of a spy in their midst and a counterattack staged by Oldsmobile, the fleet, having underestimated the enemy, is plunged into a desperate struggle.

The year is U.C. 0123.
Frontier IV, one of the colonies of the burgeoning colony cluster, Frontier Side, is suddenly invaded by a mysterious mobile suit squad.
The colony is plunged into chaos by the conflict between this squad, champions of the Crossbone Vanguard, and the station Federation Forces.
In an effort to escape the mayhem of the battle, Seabook Arno, a local high school student, flees the colony along with his younger sister, Rees, and several other students.
Amidst the confusion, one such student, Cecily Fairchild, goes missing.
Without even the time to search for her, Seabook and the others escape the colony aboard a space boat.
Around this time, Cecily manages to reunite with her grandfather, Meitza Ronah, and her father, Carozzo Ronah, who has changed his name to Iron Mask.
Faced with the fact that she is a member of the Ronah family to whom the Crossbone Vanguard answers, Cecily is racked with anguish.

Published in Monthly Shonen Ace between 1994 and 1997, this manga was drawn by Yuichi Hasegawa and based on a concept from Yoshiyuki Tomino.
Set in U.C. 133, 10 years after the events of the theatrical anime Mobile Suit Gundam F91, it depicts the battle between the Crossbone Vanguard and the Jupiter Empire.

The year is U.C. 0153. A century and a half after humanity expanded and took up residence in space, an age of turbulence termed the "Space Warring States Era" approaches the Earth Sphere.
Amidst the instability, Uso Ewin and Shakti Kareen, residents of the illegal residence area Point Kasarelia in Eastern Europe, become entangled in a battle between the Zanscare Empire, a colony nation that invaded Earth, and the organization resisting them—the League Militaire, thrusting them into the larger conflict. Uso, having become the pilot of the Victory Gundam in an effort to protect those around him, heads into space for the first time in his life.
There, he meets the children of Highland, but also loses a great many people he cares about.

If you like, please allow me to explain to you what G Gundam is about...
60 years ago.
Humanity abandoned the frittered away Earth  and moved to space to start a new life.
And the people who left for space came up with a sport called the Gundam Fight to prevent wars between colony nations.
It is now 60 years into Future Century...
The 13th Gundam Fight Tournament will now begin.
However, the tournament seems to be a little different this year.
Now, what kind of storm lies ahead of us...?
Everyone say it with me!
Gundam Fight! Ready...GO!

The year is After Colony 195. The space colonies decide to carry out Operation Meteor, a plan involving the deployment of Gundam Mobile Suits to Earth against the United Earth Sphere Alliance, which is trying to bring the colonies under its rule by force.
When she ends up witnessing an unmasked Gundam pilot, Heero Yuy, Relena Darlian is sucked into this vortex of fate.
As Heero and four other Gundam pilots establish contact as if drawn to each other, Treize Khushrenada, the Commander of the secret society OZ, sets a plan into motion to step onto the stage of history...

A story based on the TV anime Mobile Suit Gundam Wing, based on a new scenario written by Katsuyuki Sumisawa, in which author Tomofumi Ogasawara retells the story of the TV anime from a different perspective.
The Mobile Suit designs in this manga are based on the ones redone by Hajime Katoki for the OVA Mobile Suit Gundam Wing: Endless Waltz.

The year is After Colony 195.
The secret society OZ, which has taken its place on the stage of history, has expanded from Earth to space and calls for the liberation of the colonies.
Around the same time, new Mobile Suits are being developed in secret on the mining asteroid MO-V.
Odin Barnet, an MO-V test pilot, longs to become a pilot for OZ, but one day his dream is shattered in an unspeakable way...

The year is After Colony 196.
It has been one year since the battle between the Earth Sphere United Nation and the revolutionary army White Fang engulfed space in war.
To achieve a lasting peace, the people who established the Earth Sphere United Nation take steps to dismantle the forces and destroy the weapons of war remaining in the Earth Sphere.
While these efforts are underway, Vice Foreign Minister Relena Darlian is kidnapped during a visit to the colony L-3 X18999.
The lost 13th Constellation and the true Operation Meteor...
Little does anyone suspect that the threat of a new war is casting its shadow over Earth...

Following one colony's independence movement, full-blown war between the Earth and its space colonies violently errupts.
In the aftermath of these events, the vast majority of Earth's 10 billion-strong population and all its settlements have been totally devastated.
15 years pass and the global environment finally enters a state of stability. A war orphan, Garrod Ran, emerges from the mess with refined knowledge and skills for survival through desperate times. During a job request, he meets Tiffa Adill, a girl possessing Newtype abilities.
This series, set against the backdrop of the Moon and her wilderness, recounts the story of boy and girl's growth within this newly emerging world.

The year is Correct Century 2345. The Resettlement Operation is initiated by the Moonrace. 
Loran Cehack descends to Earth as an observer, familiarizes himself with life on Earth and lives peacefully.
However, on the day of his coming-of-age ceremony, the Moonrace's Return team begins to descend upon Earth and launches their first attacks on towns with Mobile Suits, thus igniting a violent conflict between the Earth and the Moon.
To stop the war, Loran pilots the Machine Doll, surfaced from a stone statue called White Doll, and expels the Moonrace's army.
Loran, who later becomes the designated pilot of White Doll (Turn A Gundam) begins his mission to reconcile the two sides: ""The Moonrace Settlers"" and ""The Earthrace Resistance.""

The year is Cosmic Era 70.
The world is divided between normal humans called Natural and genetically modified humans called Coordinators.
The "Bloody Valentine" tragedy causes tensions between them to explode, and the situation soon escalates into a full-scale armed conflict.
Everyone expects the Alliance forces to prevail with their superior numbers. But these hopes are dashed, and the war has dragged on for over eleven months.
The mining asteroid Heliopolis floats at Lagrange 5. There, Kira Yamato, a student enrolled at a technical college, encounters ZAFT forces invading the colony to steal new Mobile Suits being manufactured for the Alliance forces.
Included among the ZAFT soldiers involved in the operation is Athrun Zala, who was once Kira's best friend...
This series is a collection of additional weapon and character variations that did not appear in the anime Mobile Suit Gundam SEED.

ASTRAY... Those who have gone their own way.
This is another story that plays out behind the scenes of the great war between the Naturals and Coordinators in the year 70 of the Cosmic Era.
The protagonists are the young man Lowe Guele, who collects and repairs junk for the Junk Guild, and the mercenary Gai Murakumo, who lives in the shadow of war.
The two of them obtained new Mobile Suits from the mining asteroid Heliopolis, which was destroyed by the ZAFT forces. And their story begins here...

This is one more story that played out behind the scenes of the great war between the Naturals and Coordinators in the year 70 of the Cosmic Era.
"R" mainly tells the story with Lowe Guele as the protagonist.
Lowe's lifestyle and approach as a member of the non-militant Junk Guild also have various effects on others living in these turbulent times...

This is one more story that played out behind the scenes of the great war between the Naturals and Coordinators in the year 70 of the Cosmic Era.
"B" mainly tells the story with Gai Murakumo as the protagonist.
As a member of the mercenary unit Serpent Tail, Gai takes on special missions that the regular army cannot handle.
As he completes one mission after another with his companions, the secrets of his birth are revealed.

A Gundam suddenly appears before Lowe Guele of the Junk Guild and his companions.
Canard Pars, a young man who says he is searching for Kira Yamato, attacks Lowe and the others without warning and then leaves.
After that, Lowe and the others take on a new job, but the client's ship is attacked by Serpent Tail, and the head of the Gundam they are supposed to deliver is stolen.
Prayer Reverie, the young representative aboard the ship, asks Lowe and the others to recover the stolen head.

In C.E. 71, the year-and-a-half-long war between the Earth Alliance and PLANT came to a tentative close following the deaths of both of their leaders.
Although peace returned to the world for a time, the Earth Alliance and PLANT failed to finalize a cease-fire agreement, creating a tinderbox that could ignite into war at any moment.
Amidst the tensions created by the temporary truce, an "Avenger" appeared and threatened to disturb its delicate balance.

The year is Cosmic Era 70.
After the vicious war between ZAFT and the Earth Alliance triggered by the "Bloody Valentine" tragedy culminates in the Second Battle of Jachin Due, which results in great sacrifices, the two sides conclude a cease-fire agreement.
But this is not enough to completely extinguish the fires of war...
During the ensuing conflict, Shinn Asuka gets caught up in the invasion of Orb by the Earth Alliance forces, and he witnesses the deaths of his parents and little sister. 
Heartbroken, he departs Orb and heads to the PLANTs...
In year 73 of the Cosmic Era, Shinn finds himself piloting a Gundam as a ZAFT soldier...
This series is a collection of additional weapon and character variations that did not appear in the anime Mobile Suit Gundam SEED Destiny.

As the world is once again divided in the struggle between ZAFT and the Earth Alliance, a smaller battle is about to begin behind the scenes.
Though small now, if the battle is mishandled, it threatens to drag the entire world into warfare.
It all begins when the mysterious organization Librarian appears before Lowe Guele of the Junk Guild and demands that he surrender everything.

It is the Cosmic Era, during which multiple wars have stormed through the Earth Sphere and come to an end.
This story begins when Rondo Mina Sahaku, who sent out the Heavenly Decree during past hostilities, selects two young women, Kazahana Azure and Lath Winslet, as candidates to be her successor.

In C.E. 75, the fighting still continues.
There are independence movements, and aggression by Blue Cosmos... In order to calm the situation, a global peace monitoring agency called COMPASS is established, with Lacus as its first president.
As members of COMPASS, Kira and his comrades intervene into various regional battles. Then a newly established nation called Foundation proposes a joint operation against a Blue Cosmos stronghold.

The year is 2307 A.D.
Earth's fossil fuels have been depleted, but humanity has obtained a new energy source: a large-scale solar power system supported by three massive orbital elevators.
However, the only ones who can benefit from this system are the major powers and their allies.
These superpowers include the Union, to which the United States of America belongs, the Human Reform League, to which China, Russia, and India belong, and the AEU, to which Europe belongs.
As a result of each superpower vying for prestige and prosperity, a zero-sum game continues even now in the 24th century. 
In response to this world, a private military organization that seeks to eradicate war appears.
The organization, which utilizes mobile weapons called Gundams, is called Celestial Being.
They have started responding to all acts of war with armed intervention using their Gundams...

Gundams, independently developed mobile weapons.
Celestial Being, which owns them, has spent a long time developing them.
Some, sympathetic to the ideas of the genius scientist Aeolia Schenberg, sacrifice their youth to the development of Gundams.
Some dispute the scouting of the Gundam Meisters who will pilot Gundams in preparation for full-scale armed interventions.
This is the story of the second-generation Gundam Meisters, and it covers the events preceding the appearance of Celestial Being.

It is early in the 23rd century.
As the world starts to head toward unification under the Earth Federation, the A-LAWS begin launching their attacks.
Certain people who have blended into the population and serve as Veda's eyes in the world suddenly awaken. 
These people, who have lived ordinary lives until now, are flung into a far removed new world.
Leif Recitativo, the first person to awaken, sets out on a journey with Telicyra Herfi in search of six comrades.
But what fate lies in store for those who have awakened?

Celestial Being is a private military organization that suddenly appeared in the world. They own mobile weapons called Gundams, which they use to intervene in disputes in an effort to end war.
However, the world is divided between Celestial Being and the three nations that oppose them.
This story reveals the history of the wars and mobile weapons underlying this era of upheaval from the perspective of the Mobile Suit-development historical researcher Robert Spacey.

The Advanced Generation, where hundreds of years have passed since the migration to the space colonies...
The war over hegemony finally ends. 
Just when humanity thought that peace would come, the dream is instantly shattered to pieces.
The Unknown Enemy (UE) suddenly appeared and destroyed the Angel, one of the space colonies... This catastrophe is later named The Day the Angel Fell.
Since then, the intensity of the fight between UE and the Earth Federation Forces grows everyday, and Flit Asuno got caught in the fire of the battle and lost his mother.
A memory unit, AGE Device that is passed down in the Asuno family was entrusted to Flit by his mother. 
Using the blueprints in it, he decides to make the ancient savior Mobile Suit, Gundam.
Now, the story of Gundam over 100 years and three generations begins...

Two paths that were never meant to cross... One, belonging to Asemu Asuno, the son of Earth Federation Forces commander Flit Asuno. The other, to Zeheart Galette, a warrior who took up arms for the people of Vagan. They spend fleeting moments together, bonding and occasionally clashing, but always gazing up at the same stars.
And yet, an ironic twist of fate comes between them. Is this chance meeting between two boys the beginning of a tragedy or a glimmer of hope?
In the empty space between the stars, they cross blades, shout each other's names, and shed tears. Two souls, caught up in war, marching inexorably toward the same future... Here and now, their fate unfolds.
A spin-off published in Monthly Hobby Japan from July 2012 to May 2013.
An MSV project further expanding the world of Mobile Suit Gundam AGE, serialized in the ASCII Media Works model magazine "Dengeki Hobby Magazine" from July 2012 to June 2013.

A short period after the end of the Universal Century, humanity welcomes a new era, the Regild Century (RC).
The year is R.C. 1014. Capital Tower is a sacred place where Earth's primary energy source, Photon Batteries brought over from space are stored.
Bellri Zenam, a candidate for Capital Guard appointed to protect the Capital Tower, is attacked by space pirates during his initial training.
Bellri, who retaliates with Construction Mobile Suit Recten, succeeds in capturing his opponent's high-performance Mobile Suit G-Self with his skillful maneuvering technique. Moreover, he manages to activate G-Self, which can only be achieved under particular conditions.
This series details the motives behind the space pirates who attacked Capital Tower, the mysterious G-Self, and Bellri himself in face of the unimaginable truth that lies ahead...
This is only the beginning of Reconguista!

Roughly 300 years have passed since the end of the great conflict known as the Calamity War.
The Earth Sphere has lost its former governing structure, and, although a temporary peace has formed thanks to the new system of government there, a new conflict is about to begin far away from Earth, in the Mars Sphere.
This series recounts the tale of Kudelia Aina Bernstein, a girl trying to get to the Earth Sphere to aid in a movement to secure the independence of Mars, Mikazuki Augus and the other young soldiers who have accepted the mission to escort her there, and Gjallarhorn, a powerful military organization intent on preserving world peace...
This is the story of their earnest efforts to survive even as their goals and ideals clash against the backdrop of a chaotic and unstable world."

Another Orphans story is about to begin.
The boss of the mafia organization Tanto Tempo, which controls the Avalanche Colony located near the Moon, has been assassinated.
The boss has been succeeded by his only daughter, Liarina Morugaton.
However, many people bent on claiming her enormous power for themselves take steps to try to kill her.
Due to a strange twist of fate, Argi Mirage and Volco Warren end up with the job of protecting her, and they commit themselves to ensuring that she stays in charge.
One is secretly motivated by the desire to destroy the Gundam that killed his family, while the other hopes to rebuild the Gundam left by his.
They both hold their true plans close to their hearts...

P.D. 0323. An incident in which Gjallarhorn intervenes with Arbrau central government politics, leading to the utilization of mobile suits as a means of military force.
The incident is brought to an end by Tekkadan, a group of young men from Mars.
Word of Tekkadan's exploits reach Wistario Afam, a boy born and raised on Radonitsa, a colony orbiting Venus.
Having lost out on development opportunities to Mars, Venus is a remote planet of little interest to the four economic blocs.
Its residents are without IDs, and it is used solely as a penal colony.
While harboring hopes of turning his hometown around, Wistario finds himself faced with a young girl who claims to be a guide to something called the ""Urdr-Hunt.""
The Mobile Suit Gundam Iron-Blooded Orphans MSV project showcases mobile suits from across the franchise, beyond the TV series and spin-off Mobile Suit Gundam Iron-Blooded Orphans Gekko.

At Fólkvangr, a front constructed inside an asteroid, the Gundam Lfrith is undergoing operating tests in the Vanadis Institute's laboratory. The Lfrith has not yet managed to meet the conditions imposed by the council, and test pilot Elnora Samaya is growing impatient.
That same day, her daughter is celebrating her fourth birthday...

A.S. (Ad Stella) 122. An era when a multitude of corporations have entered space and built a huge economic system. A lone girl from the remote planet Mercury transfers to the Asticassia School of Technology, run by the Benerit Group which dominates the mobile suit industry. Her name is Suletta Mercury. With a scarlet light burning in her pure heart, this girl walks step by step through a new world.

While visiting Odaiba, Tokyo, in the summer of 2009, Haru has a life-changing encounter with the enormous, life-sized Gundam statue in Shiokaze Park. After coming into a model kit named BEGINNING GUNDAM by pure chance, Haru finds himself thrilled to be putting together his first ever model. He learns that finished models can be pitted against one another in Gunpla Battles, and decides to try a battle for himself. This proves to be Haru's first step down the path to becoming a Gunpla Builder...

In the near future...
The 1980's saw an immense wave of popularity in the Mobile Suit Gundam plastic models, or Gunpla.
And now, the second Gunpla wave is rising up again.
The driving force behind this are Gunpla battles.
Instead of CG in virtual space, these are fights between controlled, built Gunpla. 
Its popularity skyrocketed with this revolutionary battle system, and there is even an annual World Tournament.
Sei Iori is a young Gunpla builder who loves Gunpla.
Reiji is a mysterious Gunpla builder with excellent piloting skills.
The meeting between these two opens the door to the Gunpla Battle World Tournament...

A manga written by Koichi Yamato, and published between 1982 and 1986 in Kodansha's Comic BomBom magazine.
The story follows the plastic model-loving Shiro Kyoda (who goes by the nickname Plamo Kyoshiro) and his experience with the Plamo Simulation Machine that allows him to battle with plastic models, and depicts both his battles with rivals and his growth as a modeler.

It is the warring states era, a period of strife.
Following his sudden appearance, the Dark Shogun joins forces with the Zaku Clan to form the Darkness Corps and declare war on the Gundam Forces, themselves once allies of the Zaku. 
In response to this crisis, Shogundam sets about gathering the Seven Mushas from across the land. 
He is able to assemble only five, however, and so, with these Five Mushas leading the charge, the battle against the Darkness Corps begins.

In an era removed from our own, there is a place known as the Saddrac World. In this world, humans and mobile suits live in peace...
One day, however, accompanied by a flash of lightning, a gigantic shadow looms suddenly over the land. This shadow belongs to the Satan Gundam, the Demon King of Zeon.
The Demon King declares his intentions to rid the world of humans and create a place for mobile suits alone to live.
His evil influence extends as far the remote Lacroa Kingdom, wherein Revil, King of Lacroa, chooses a single hero and orders him to save the very world. The name of this hero is Knight Gundam, and his adventure starts here...

In pursuit of the evil biomechanical lifeform Fuhrer, the Command Gundam travels to the central continent Magellan and begins training the local mobile suits.
Enlisting their help, he forms G-ARMS in an effort to resist Fuhrer and combat the Zatarion Legion in this, the first of the SD Command Chronicles.

It is an era in which the landscapes of once-proud nations are now strewn with mountains of debris wrought by countless conflicts.
Just as the light of hope begins to shine anew, a mysterious threat in the form of the Etiolation Trinity spreads to every corner of the globe.
Moreover, Bugs, beings that have lost their sense of self, begin attacking mobile suits one after another.
In order to escape the Bugs' assault, the surviving units are left with no choice but to live out their lives inside city strongholds.
With the former lord gone, however, the city residents must live under the tyrannical regime of the new lord, Dong Zhuo.
As if guided by fate, Liu Bei Unicorn Gundam encounters Guan Yu V Gundam and Zhang Fei God Gundam, and resolves to save the world.
Spurred on by the passion of this trio, global change is finally set in motion. The time has come for these all-new heroes to bring about a new age!

A war simulation game released by Bandai on November 20th, 1987.
A number of related titles would be released following the release of the sequel "SD Gundam World Gachapon Senshi 2 - Capsule Wars," on March 3rd 1989.
The title of a series of short movies made and released between 2001 and 2007, these movies combine new mecha action with stories that run parallel to popular existing Gundam works.

This series of competitive simulation games was preceded by SUPER GACHAPON WORLD SD Gundam X, which Bandai released on September 18th, 1992. It includes SD Gundam GX, which was released on May 27th, 1994; SD Gundam GNEXT, which was released on December 22nd, 1995; and SD Gundam GCENTURY, which was released on March 20th, 1997.

This competitive fighting game was made by Bandai and released on June 20th, 1997.
Its follow-up, Gundam: The Battle Master 2, was released on March 12th, 1998, and as Gundam: Battle Assault on November 6th, 2000 in the US.
A 1996 real-time war strategy game released by Bandai, on September 19th, 2001, it was rereleased under the name Gundam Tactics Mobility Fleet 0079.

A war simulation game released by Bandai on April 9th, 1998. 
A number of related titles would be released following the debut of the sequel, Mobile Suit Gundam: Gihren's Greed - Blood of Zeon, on February 10, 2000.

A dome-screen team fighting game released by Bandai Namco on November 7th, 2006. 
Rev. 2.00 would be released on December 8th, 2008; Rev. 3.00 would be released on July 29th, 2011.

A team-battle action game released and operated by Bandai Namco, this is the ninth entry in the Mobile Suit Gundam VS. series.
Service started in 2010 and the game was released for home consoles via the PlayStation 3 in 2011.

This series of war simulation games was preceded by the SD Gundam Generation series, which Bandai released on July 26th, 1996. Since SD Gundam G Generation was released on August 6th, 1998, many games in the series have been released, including this one.
